title: Why didnt they bring in Unit 02 to fight Sachiel
display_title: Why didnt they bring in Unit 02 to fight Sachiel
wikitext:
[]: Why didn't they bring in Unit 02 to fight Sachiel?             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Wed Feb 23, 2011 10:12 pm This falls into guessing territory, but hear me out here. IIRC, Eva-02 was already completed before Sachiel came calling, albeit being on the other side of the world. Gendo and Fuyutsuki knew Sachi was coming before he even showed up. Why didn't they bring in the more advanced Eva to deal with him instead of calling in Shinji and sticking him in Eva-01?

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Wed Feb 23, 2011 10:17 pm The point of the episode was to introduce Unit 01 and develop key aspect of Shinji's character. Bringing a second Evangelion into the show so soon would have detracted from that. Edit: Similarly, it would go against Gendo's scenario to bring in Unit 02 as its presence so early in the story would make Shinji redundant - Gendo's scenario is to use Shinji in Unit 01 so that the Shinji/Yui synergy can ultimately subvert Seele's scenario and achieve Gendo's own version instrumentality.

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Wed Feb 23, 2011 10:22 pm That's the IRL reason, but I was just thinking if there was an in-story reason for it...

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Thu Feb 24, 2011 1:10 am Unit 02 is useless as far as Gendou's concerned. It rankles him even to have to involve Shinji in his plans - he wanted Rei, piloting Unit 01, and not a whole lot else to get in the way. Also, it's not clear to what extent Gendou was able to control the actions of Nerv's German branch - while NGE doesn't have the same kind of inter-branch power plays as Rebuild does, it's also suggested several times that other branches of Nerv are more tightly under SEELE's control than the Tokyo-3 HQ is. Also, it's probably a time-consuming and expensive process to move an Eva halfway across the world, and it requires the compliance of the entire UN Navy, apparently. If Gendou had requested that Unit 02 be transferred to Japan (assuming he was allowed to request such a thing) he would surely have been asked why, and "uhh...i have a bad feeling about aliens" is not exactly the sort of thing you want to tell military brass when they want explanations. He already had two Evas, one pilot at the ready (or so he hoped), and another who could be utilized at reasonably short notice: why bother with importing another damn Eva and another damn pilot, neither of which you care about?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Feb 24, 2011 8:26 am They knew Angels were coming but they didn't know the exact time. Gendo didn't really know when Sachiel exactly was going to attack

[]: Re: Why didn't they bring in Unit 02 to fight Sachiel?             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Feb 24, 2011 12:22 pm CyberXIII wrote: This falls into guessing territory, but hear me out here. IIRC, Eva-02 was already completed before Sachiel came calling, albeit being on the other side of the world. Gendo and Fuyutsuki knew Sachi was coming before he even showed up. Why didn't they bring in the more advanced Eva to deal with him instead of calling in Shinji and sticking him in Eva-01?

[]: Re: Why didn't they bring in Unit 02 to fight Sachiel?             Quote Post by Azathoth » Thu Feb 24, 2011 12:32 pm Bagheera wrote: A better question asks why he didn't train Shinji to pilot Unit 01 beforehand, particularly given what he knew of Unit 02 and its pilot.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Feb 24, 2011 2:13 pm Shinji wasn't brought in until the last minute because Gendo wanted Rei to pilot Unit 01, but then she got injured. "Well fuck."

[]: Re: Why didn't they bring in Unit 02 to fight Sachiel?             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Feb 24, 2011 2:58 pm Azathoth wrote: Because he didn't want Shinji to be involved at all, and the expectation was apparently that Unit 01 herself do most of the heavy lifting, even when Rei was the one ostensibly piloting.

[]: Re: Why didn't they bring in Unit 02 to fight Sachiel?             Quote Post by Azathoth » Thu Feb 24, 2011 3:10 pm Bagheera wrote: Except that didn't work, and he knew why it didn't work, and he knew he'd need two pilots eventually anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Feb 24, 2011 3:24 pm This question reminds me of the episode in Lost where Locke tells Charlie about the moth, and how he could help it emerge from its cocoon, but then it would surely die from being too weak. Struggle is nature's way of strengthening. Narrative structure aside, if Asuka just showed up in Unit-02 and defeated the angels for him, Shinji would not have had to struggle and grow stronger in the process. But mostly, it wouldn't be good storytelling.

[]: Re: Why didn't they bring in Unit 02 to fight Sachiel?             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Feb 24, 2011 3:39 pm Azathoth wrote: He hadn't had a chance to test Rei in Unit 01 yet, owing to Unit 00 being a total piece of shit.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Feb 24, 2011 3:40 pm I think Gendo wanted to keep Shinji out of the whole thing, maybe in a twisted attempt to protect him (Yui DID want him on the project, tough we can only speculate whether Gendo knew that) But then, Rei injured herself and Gendo knew that only one person in the world could be expected to reliably synch with Yui's soul on the first attempt: Her own kid. He's a pragmatist, after all. As for Asuka - Rebuild gives us the explanation that Unit 02 was still being tested. If they "corrected" this in Rebuild, it might've been a plot hole they overlooked and later noticed. On the other hand, Gendo & co might not have known WHEN EXACTLY the Angels would strike - Him & Fuyu were never shown looking at clocks/checking the time. Another possibility is that Gendo needed Unit 02's transport to guard/conceal the transport of Adam.

[]: Re: Why didn't they bring in Unit 02 to fight Sachiel?             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Feb 24, 2011 4:00 pm Bagheera wrote: Well, yes, but relying on that with no internal rationale for the decision is also weak storytelling. You gotta sell it. And it's not like it'd be hard -- something like "At the time Unit 02 was undergoing a refit, and as such wasn't available. It was made ready and shipped over as soon as possible under the circumstances." would do the trick.

[]: Re: Why didn't they bring in Unit 02 to fight Sachiel?             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Feb 24, 2011 4:26 pm Kendrix wrote: I think Gendo wanted to keep Shinji out of the whole thing, maybe in a twisted attempt to protect him (Yui DID want him on the project, tough we can only speculate whether Gendo knew that) But then, Rei injured herself and Gendo knew that only one person in the world could be expected to reliably synch with Yui's soul on the first attempt: Her own kid. He's a pragmatist, after all.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Feb 24, 2011 4:29 pm NERV is paramilitary organization equipped with weapons, soldiers etc. they run defence systems of Tokyo-3 in the first place, meaning they have tons of firepower and soldiers in their command. I have no idea why you're being so asspained about this. Geez. Civilian organizations do not have the kind of material NERV has access to nor is purpose of any civilian organization combat. NERV's purpose (one of them) is to fight against Angels. That NERV's ranks do not follow general army ranking systems just shows it is distinct from traditional armies like JSSDF

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Feb 24, 2011 5:12 pm Xard wrote: NERV is paramilitary organization equipped with weapons, soldiers etc. they run defence systems of Tokyo-3 in the first place, meaning they have tons of firepower and soldiers in their command. I have no idea why you're being so asspained about this. Geez.

[]: Re: Why didn't they bring in Unit 02 to fight Sachiel?             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Feb 24, 2011 5:15 pm Bagheera wrote: narrative structure isn't enough to justify a story element -- it has to make sense internally as well. If it doesn't it amounts to a plot hole.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Feb 24, 2011 5:15 pm It's always been my impression that paramilitary leans more heavily to the "military" than "civilian" side of things but I admit I may be wrong about this. And what doesn't bear under scrutiny here exactly? What is wrong about NERV's nature? Why is there something questionable about its structure as special agency created to fight against Angels and to furthen SEELE's plans? It works perfectly apart from Gendo's hijinks as far as SEELE is considered I don't see any problem here on any level.

[]: Re: Why didn't they bring in Unit 02 to fight Sachiel?             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Feb 24, 2011 5:23 pm Bagheera wrote: "At the time Unit 02 was undergoing a refit, and as such wasn't available. It was made ready and shipped over as soon as possible under the circumstances." would do the trick.
